title: document how to enable dns in default netavark network
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I want to enable dns resolution in the default network created by (rootful) podman.  I did not find documentation on how to do that.

Ideally it would also include the name of the configfiles to use to allow easy autoprovisioning via ignition file (without having to create `OneShot`-Units).


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.18beta2

Built:      Thu Mar  3 14:56:09 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
Hi @ibotty , Thanks for creating the issue. 
Afaik `dns` on default network is always disabled and it was like this to match docker compatibility.
Sorry, I was talking about netavark.  Thank you for the link.  I'll try to create a pull request to add it to the FAQ.
I did not find a FAQ.  Is the basic networking file the right place?
Yes that should be a good place.
